author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #579, which landed GPU dispatch for the four core 1-D FFT ops (\`fft\`, \`ifft\`, \`rfft\`, \`irfft\`).

## Remaining surface for full FFT GPU coverage

### A. hfft / ihfft (Hermitian FFT, 1-D)
\`hfft(x)\` = ifft of conjugate-symmetric input × N (real output)
\`ihfft(x)\` = rfft with conjugated output (real input → Hermitian-symmetric complex)

Both reduce to existing cuFFT primitives + a sign-flip kernel. Wire on top of the existing \`gpu_irfft_c2r_*\` / \`gpu_rfft_r2c_*\`.

### B. 2-D and N-D variants
\`fft2\` / \`ifft2\` / \`rfft2\` / \`irfft2\` use \`cufftPlan2d\` directly with appropriate type.
\`fftn\` / \`ifftn\` / \`rfftn\` / \`irfftn\` use \`cufftPlan3d\` for the 3-D cases or \`plan_many\` for arbitrary rank with strides.

Add to \`ferrotorch-gpu/src/cufft.rs\` parallel to existing 1-D wrappers, then trait + dispatch.

### C. Padded / truncated \`n\`
The 1-D ops only dispatch to GPU when \`fft_n == input_n\`. When the user passes \`n != input_n\`, dispatch currently falls back to CPU (with a comment). Two options:
1. Allocate a zeroed GPU buffer of the right size and \`memcpy_dtod\` the input portion (per-batch loop, simple).
2. Use \`cufftPlanMany\` with \`idist\` / \`odist\` set so cuFFT reads only the first \`n\` real samples per batch (truncation only — padding still needs the alloc-and-copy).

Most usage is \`n=None\`, so this is genuinely tail work.

### D. Layout: real-input FFT shape compat
\`rfftn\` over multiple axes requires careful shape bookkeeping (the last axis becomes \`n/2+1\`, others stay full). Verify against torch's \`fft.rfftn\` shape convention before wiring.
